results.label: Standard — woodworking tutorial
results.output_summary: Generated 26 shots organized by script section. Shot types varied: WIDE, CLOSE-UP, INSERT, MEDIUM, TIME-LAPSE SUGGESTION. Each shot specific: '[CLOSE-UP] Glue bead along board edge, slight squeeze-out visible as clamps tighten.' Shoot Day Notes group by setup: raw lumber shots first, process shots in order, hero/finished product shots last.

A1.note: Numbered shots under bold section headers, shot type in brackets, subject description, purpose note.

A2.note: Descriptions are filmable without interpretation. Shot types varied across WIDE, CLOSE-UP, INSERT, MEDIUM, TIME-LAPSE.

A3.note: Shoot Day Notes section present with logical groupings.

results.label: Minimal — only required fields
results.output_summary: Generated 16 shots for a shorter, simpler video. Shot types appropriately limited to what's achievable at a desk. Each shot specific to the keyboard cleaning process. Shoot Day Notes minimal but present.

A1.note: Correct format throughout. Proportionally fewer shots for a simpler topic.

A2.note: Specific descriptions. Shot types varied within the constraints of a desk setup.

A3.note: Shoot Day Notes present, groups setup/before shots separately from process shots.

results.label: European context — Italian pasta making tutorial
results.output_summary: Generated 30 shots emphasizing hand technique and texture per the Italian kitchen aesthetic. Macro lens noted as available and incorporated for dough texture close-ups. Cultural details preserved: mattarello technique, tipo 00 flour, Parmigiano. Shoot Day Notes group by kitchen setup stages. Flags that natural light from the window should be checked before filming.

A1.note: Full format with section headers matching the script. Purpose notes explain editorial intent.

A2.note: Highly specific: '[MACRO] Flour dust rising as hands fold dough on wooden board — tactile texture detail.' Varied shot types including macro.

A3.note: Shoot Day Notes group by setup stage and flag natural light timing.
